_comment: This file is generated. Please edit .homeycompose/app.json instead.

description.en: Power control and cheaper-hour scheduling for EV charging, heating and hot water.
description.no: Effektstyring og bruk av billige timer for elbillading, varme og varmtvann.

title.en: Hard cap breach imminent — manual action needed

titleFormatted.en: Hard cap breach imminent — no managed devices left to limit

hint.en: Fires when PELS expects this hour to exceed the hard cap and every managed device is already turned off or at its lowest step. Use it to alert someone to turn something off by hand. Daily budget overruns do not fire this trigger.

Fires once when a smart task ends. The [[outcome]] tag tells you how it ended — succeeded (target reached and held), missed (ready-by time passed before reaching the target), or abandoned (cleared by hand or the device dropped out). Values are stable lowercase ids — filter on them downstream. The [[shortfall_known]] flag is false when the device-side delta was not observable and [[shortfall]] fell back to 0; gate any numeric comparison on it.

Fires when the smart task is rescheduled with a different number of hours — for example when new prices arrive, or when the device runs faster than estimated. Hour swaps that keep the same total and kWh-only redistributions stay quiet.
Fires when the smart task status transitions for the chosen device. Filter on the [[status]] tag downstream; values are stable lowercase ids: waiting / on_track / at_risk / unachievable / satisfied.

titleFormatted.en: PELS wants [[device]] at a different step

hint.en: Fires when PELS decides a stepped device should move to a different configured step. Use it in flows that translate PELS intent into a vendor-specific action.
Use this to trigger flows when PELS switches to a different operating mode.

title.en: Current price is one of the lowest before a time

titleFormatted.en: Current price is one of [[number]] lowest in the [[period]] hours before [[time]]:00

hint.en: Counted back from the end hour. The window can cross midnight — for example, 6 hours before 04:00 searches 22:00 the previous day through 03:59.
Ties at the cutoff price are included.
Hour the window ends at, in local time (use 24 for midnight).

title.en: Current price is one of today's lowest

titleFormatted.en: Current price is one of [[number]] lowest prices today

hint.en: Ties at the cutoff price are included.

title.en: Smart task time is running low

titleFormatted.en: [[device]] smart task has [[hours]] hours or fewer remaining

hint.en: Fires once when the hours remaining until the smart task's Ready by drops to the chosen value or fewer. Re-arms when the Ready by is rescheduled. Use it to act a fixed lead-time before a deadline (e.g. 2 hours or fewer). The [[hours_remaining]] tag carries the whole hours remaining at the crossing.
True when the current smart task status for this device matches the chosen status.
True when this device has a smart task stored in PELS.

titleFormatted.en: Is there enough available power for [[required_kw]] kW?

hint.en: Use this to check if the current available power is enough to add the requested load.

title.en: Is there available power for device?

titleFormatted.en: Does [[device]] have available power for [[required_kw]] kW?

hint.en: True when the house has room for this device to draw the requested kW. PELS waits about 60 seconds after limiting the device, and asks for extra room when recent attempts to bring it back already fell short — so flows do not flap the device on and off.
Use this to create flows that only run when PELS is in a specific mode (e.g., Home, Away, Night).
Use this to check if the selected device is ignored by daily budget control while still counting in real usage.

title.en: Is power-limit control enabled for device?

hint.en: Use this to check whether PELS may limit or resume the selected device to stay under the hard cap.
Use this to check if PELS is currently managing the selected device.

title.en: Current price is one of the lowest before a time

titleFormatted.en: Current price is one of [[number]] lowest in the [[period]] hours before [[time]]:00

hint.en: Counted back from the end hour. The window can cross midnight — for example, 6 hours before 04:00 searches 22:00 the previous day through 03:59.
Ties at the cutoff price are included.
Hour the window ends at, in local time (use 24 for midnight).

title.en: Current price is one of today's lowest

titleFormatted.en: Current price is one of [[number]] lowest prices today

hint.en: Ties at the cutoff price are included.
Exclude this device from daily budget control. Real usage still counts in charts and budget reporting.

title.en: Set what a smart task may do

hint.en: Choose what PELS may do to keep this smart task's deadline, and when — go over today's budget, or limit lower-priority devices so this task gets the power it needs. 'While it's scheduled to run' applies it the whole time the task is active; the change takes effect on the next plan refresh and sticks until you change it or clear the smart task.
Removes the active smart task for this device.
Keep PELS from limiting or resuming this device while power-limit control is disabled.
Allow PELS to limit or resume this device to stay under the hard cap.
Make this device follow daily budget control again.
Store battery percentage for a managed charger without requiring the charger itself to expose a battery capability.
Send the current whole-house power reading (W) from your meter so PELS can track hourly energy use and stay under the hard cap.
Use this after a vendor-specific action card, or when the device changes step outside PELS, so PELS knows which configured step is currently selected.
Use this when a device app gives you a power value instead of a step name. PELS matches the configured step by planning power. Accepts values like 1750 or 1750 W.
Use this to dynamically adjust your capacity limit based on time of day, electricity prices, or other conditions.
Use this to automatically switch PELS modes based on time, presence, or other triggers in your flows.
Use this to adjust your daily energy budget from flows. Set to 0 to disable the daily budget.

PELS plans charging from the device settings, prices, budget, and the hard cap. The smart task auto-disables once the deadline passes; run this card from a recurring Flow (e.g. when the EV is plugged in) to re-arm it for the next session.
Use this after the charger or car is already using this phase. PELS uses it to calculate charging power; it does not switch the charger hardware.

titleFormatted.en: Set expected power for [[device]] to [[power_w]] W

hint.en: Legacy/manual override of a device's expected power usage until it reports a new meter reading. Fails if the device already has load configured in settings.

titleFormatted.en: Set external prices for today from [[prices_json]]

hint.en: Paste the price JSON from Power by the Hour's "price changed" card tag. Single quotes are accepted (e.g., {'0':0.2747,'1':0.2678,...}).

titleFormatted.en: Set external prices for tomorrow from [[prices_json]]

hint.en: Paste the price JSON from Power by the Hour's "price changed" card tag. Single quotes are accepted (e.g., {'0':0.2747,'1':0.2678,'2':0.261,...}).

PELS plans heating from the device settings, prices, budget, and the hard cap. The smart task auto-disables once the deadline passes; run this card from a recurring Flow (e.g. on a daily schedule) to re-arm it for the next run.

desc.en: The current PELS operating mode. Switch directly from the device tile or via Flow.
Deprecated: shown in the daily budget plan view.
Whether the daily budget plan has been exceeded
Remaining kWh in the daily budget (can be negative)
Deprecated: daily budget usage is tracked in the Usage tab.
Number of managed devices currently limited (turned off, paused, or lowered)
Number of managed devices not currently limited
Available power before reaching the current safe pace
Current effective safe pace (minimum of hourly safe pace and daily budget pace when enabled)
kWh consumed so far this hour
Shows the current operating mode selected in settings or Flow.
Why PELS is currently limiting devices or delaying resume
Current electricity price level relative to daily average
Whether PELS projects an hourly hard cap breach and has no managed devices left to limit

insightsTitleTrue.en: Manual action needed: projected hard cap breach

insightsTitleFalse.en: Manual action no longer needed: back within hard cap
